EXPERIMENTAL FEATURE
Simultaneous use of a BLE PTT button and a Bluetooth Headset is limited by Android hardware. Some devices may drop the BLE connection.

Please do NOT buy a Bluetooth PTT speaker/mic solely for TCI Remote, as we cannot guarantee compatibility yet. We rely on community feedback to determine which devices work flawlessly. Once confirmed, we will share this info via our Announcement channel and Discord!

Credits & Acknowledgements
Acknowledgements
Thanks to Brent (N9BC) for inspiring this TCI remote app.
Thanks to the following people for their donations: G4FPH, KO6DLV, KD9QGP, K4LAV, IW6CQS, WA3O, K5JPD, K2KXK, VE1BWV, EA5AKG, KN6ZFY, W4CM, Bill Hurlock, Richard Clark, HB9HFK, Jose Vicente Ferrer, F4OQU, G0NIG, GW6IYP, Peter Albronda, WC1J, W5IUA, DL5AI, YC1SEI, DD8JM, AA0U, W4NNX, BD8AI, N3GX, DM4KCS, BG7MVS, BG7MQO, PD0MVP, IV3IDP, F4CRT, PA3CX, VK3MB, LA5BT, N4QNT, BG5FIK, BG8SVK, SV1RRP, VK3EY, EA5AKG, 7Z1WW, DL5AI, K9TDC, KC9UOE, Theo Van der Schaft.
Thanks to KO6DLV (Sonim PTT mod), AD7JL (Tune slider) & G0XBU (code for dial & more) for contributing code.
Thanks to Richie (MW0LGE) and Reid (MI0BOT) for adding the TCI protocol into Thetis.
Changelog
v3.4 — Compactor v1.3.2 Support ★ NEW
Fixed: General stability.
Fixed: Recall last frequency/mode/RX filter per band.
Fixed: Web shortcuts provided by RN radios now properly displayed per station.
Fixed: Signal meter calibration.
Added: Manual offset possible for signal meter.
Compactor: Required version updated to v1.3.2.
v3.3 — Compactor v1.3 Support
Added: New DSP controls (NB1, NB2, NB OFF, ANF, BIN) in the Noise Reduction panel.
Added: Bug & feature request link at the bottom of the page.
Improved: Custom theme reimplemented.
Fixed: Many bugs with the Radio Network implementation resolved.
Fixed: VFO swap now strictly follows band rules (<10MHz LSB, >10MHz USB).
Fixed: DXSummit issue due to remote website blacklisting.
Compactor: Full compatibility with v1.3 (Zero-Config updates & Anti-Virus fix).
v3.2 — Custom Theme & Radio Network
• Added: Radio Network — headless and transparent sharing of your radio to the Radio Network. When you come online, RN guest users will only get RX rights, depending on what has been defined in the Compactor settings.
• Added: Who's Connected now supports simple text messages being exchanged between connected RX users.
• Added: Custom Theme — fully personalise the panadapter, waterfall, and GUI colours with 13 individual colour pickers and a signal fill opacity slider.
• Added: Hide World Map Background — toggle to remove the greyline map from the panadapter; pair with a custom solid background colour for light or high-contrast themes.
• Added: Panadapter Background Colour — solid fill colour shown behind the spectrum when the world map is hidden.
• Added: Signal Fill Opacity — slider (10–100%) to control how opaque the spectrum gradient is; essential for light backgrounds where semi-transparent signals would otherwise wash out.
• Added: Live Panafall Mirror — real-time copy of the panadapter and waterfall displayed inside the Custom Theme settings panel so colour changes are visible immediately without scrolling.
• Fixed: Custom Theme mode now correctly saved and restored when the app is closed and reopened.
v3.1
• Added: Sun Mode — new display theme optimised for bright sunlight and outdoor operation. Increases contrast and colour saturation for easy reading in direct sunlight.
• Added: PTT Watchdog — critical safety feature. The TCI protocol has no built-in PTT watchdog; a dropped connection while transmitting would leave your transceiver and amplifier stuck in TX indefinitely. TCI Remote v3.1 now automatically releases PTT 15 seconds after connection loss if no recovery is detected, protecting your finals from overheating.
• Added: TUN/ATU Watchdog — automatic TUNE release timer (configurable 1–30 s) to protect your finals if the connection drops mid-tune.
• Added: Audio Status Overlay — real-time badge on the panadapter when audio is muted or RX audio is disabled.
• Added: Tunnel name shown in the TCI connection log for easier identification of active relay.
• Improved: RX filter bandwidth is now correctly restored on reconnect across all modes (LSB, USB, CW, AM, FM…).
• Fixed: All popups (AGC, web shortcuts, zoom/gain/speed/FFT sliders) now position correctly in Sun Mode and Light Mode.
• Fixed: Switching between dark, light, and sun mode no longer causes a scroll jump in the config panel.
• Security: 3 XSS vulnerabilities patched — spot data, license info, and callsign inputs are now properly sanitised.
• Added: Basic messaging — connected users can send a private message to any individual user or broadcast to everyone connected through the Compactor.
v3.0
• Added: TCI Remote Compactor — up to 93% bandwidth reduction, password-protected connection, no VPN or port forwarding needed.
• Added: Zero-Config Remote Access — connect from anywhere using callsign + PIN via automatic tunnel. No router configuration needed.
• Added: Opus audio codec — high-quality, low-latency audio over the Compactor with dramatically improved stability.
• Added: Multi-operator support — see who is connected, with built-in intercom to monitor TX audio from other operators.
• Added: TX Profile auto-switching — automatically applies your remote TX profile on connect and restores original on disconnect.
• Added: FM Mode profile — dedicated TX profile that activates automatically when switching to FM/NFM.
• Added: QoS signal quality indicator — real-time 4-bar link health display. Clickable for connection stats.
• Added: Volume amplification — AF GAIN now goes up to +15 dB for devices with low output volume.
• Added: TCP audio streaming prioritization — audio packets are given priority in the queue to reduce dropouts.
• Added: TCP audio stream packet loss behaviour — configurable: speed up audio to catch up, or mix audio streams. Toggle in Audio config.
• Added: Password control for RX, RX/TX and Master users via the Compactor proxy server.
• Added: Intercom feature — all users connected through the Compactor proxy can hear each other when transmitting.
• Added: Who is online listing — see all users currently connected through the Compactor.
• Added: Audio output routing — choose between speakers, Bluetooth, or other output devices.
• Added: Roger beep control — Off, CB only, or On all bands.
• Added: 2 additional web shortcuts in config (now 5 total).
• Added: Video recording now shows contact callsigns and station A/B info when a QSO note callsign search has been performed.
• Added: Video recording now also shows the RX bandwidth of the recording.
• Added: Comments can be added in the QSO note.
• Changed: Complete config menu rework — all settings are now organised in collapsible sections.
• Changed: Connection behaviour — tap the power button to open the server list, then tap a server to connect.
• Changed: Experimental Audio Settings menu is no longer hidden — it is now the last section in the config menu.
• Changed: Completely reworked button mapping & Bluetooth button mapping, with limitation notifications.
• Fixed: Codec implementation error resolved — humming audio streams fixed.
• Fixed: Audio stuttering issue resolved — significantly improved audio continuity during remote operation.
• Fixed: Sample rate connection control — TCI Remote now shows an error on connect if the TCI server sample rate is below 96000 Hz.
• Fixed: When PF (Panafall) is in "Always On" mode, the signal meter now correctly shows real output power during TX.
• Fixed: Removed duplicate overlay text on the panadapter when not connected.
• Fixed: Roger beep was not played back when packet loss occurred during audio streaming.
• Fixed: WhatsApp voice recordings were muted while TCI Remote was open — users no longer need to exit TCI Remote to record voice messages in WhatsApp.
• Fixed: VFO lock did not prevent all interactions — clicking spots on the panadapter or using the rotary dial could still change frequency while locked.
• Changed: CPU usage info is now displayed as an overlay in the bottom-left corner of the waterfall.
• Changed: Step rounding is now enabled by default. A toggle to enable/disable rounding has been added to the VFO A step menu.
• Added: DXSummit.com as a selectable DX Cluster spots server.
• Added: Send spots directly to DXSummit.com from within TCI Remote.
• Added: Capacitor graceful connectivity feature — smoother reconnection behaviour when the network changes or drops.
• Added: Internal website tunnelling — 5 configurable web shortcuts fed through the TCI Remote Compactor tunnel (antenna tuner, rotator, cluster, and more).
• Added: Connection quality data tester — graphical traffic monitor with RTT, jitter, packet loss and internet ping.
• And many more fixes and stability improvements.
v2.5.2
• Added: Send DX spots to DXSummit directly from the QSO Notepad — two-step submission with comment entry and confirmation.
• Added: DXSummit as selectable DX Cluster source (in addition to G7VRD and HamQTH).
• Added: TX Profile quick-switch — tap "TX PROFILE:" in the Transmit title bar to change profiles without opening Config.
• Added: Help & Tutorials section in Config with links to YouTube playlist, Thetis Config Guide, and Discord.
• Added: Station Settings section in Config with dedicated "My Callsign" field for spot submissions and ADIF export.
• Added: Video recording now shows bandpass filter info (low/high cut-off) under the S-meter.
• Added: DX Cluster spots now display comment/info text under each spot row.
• Added: Server status "Try again" button when server is offline.
• Improved: Logbook import now performs smart merge — re-importing updates comments, names, and RST fields instead of skipping duplicates.
• Improved: QRZ search can be cancelled by tapping the spinning animation.
• Improved: TX BW slider extended to 12 kHz. Bandwidth presets updated (8k→6k, 6k→5k).
• Fixed: ADIF export no longer overwrites imported QSO comments with "TCI Remote contact."
v2.5.1
• Added: TX Profile Switching — remotely select a different TX profile from the Config menu. Profile is automatically restored to the original when disconnecting.
• Added: Current TX profile name now displayed in yellow on the Transmit title bar.
• Added: AGC Gain Slider — double-click the AUTO AGC button to open a manual gain control popup. Works in both Auto and Manual AGC modes.
• Added: Hidden "Experimental Audio Settings" menu with 25+ advanced tweakable parameters for the RX/TX audio pipeline (10× click on Credits title to open).
• Added: Descriptions and default values shown under every tweak in the experimental settings.
• Added: TX Bandpass Filter presets and live filter adjustment via TCI.
• Improved: AGC Gain slider auto-disables Auto AGC when user adjusts the slider manually, with green/blue visual state feedback.
• Improved: TX Profile restoration now works across all exit scenarios (power off, app close, Thetis shutdown, restart button).
• Fixed: Suppressed chatty unknown TCI commands (agc_gain, rx_step_att_ex, tx_frequency_ex) from polluting the TCI log.
• Fixed: Removed OFF option from TX Bandpass filter presets.
v2.4
• Added: Fully integrated logbook feature in QSO note with support to recall previous contacts (Worked B4).
• Changed: Overhauled S-Meter calculation engine, introducing 3 selectable dynamic meter modes.
• Fixed: Repaired behaviour of the Always on PF when in TX.
• Fixed: Upgraded WebAudio RX pipeline to use a continuous Ring Buffer architecture to permanently fix audio ticking.
v2.2
• Added: Rotary dial popup window for tuning via "DIAL" button next to tuning slider (thanks to G0XBU).
• Fixed: Issue where popup sliders (Zoom, Gain, WF, FFT) were hidden in Light Mode.
• Changed: Server addresses can now be fully populated and managed inside the Config window.
v2.1.1
• Removed: The auto-mute feature for incoming phone calls, as it inadvertently triggered on notifications and messages (like WhatsApp) as well.
v2.1.1
• Removed: The auto-mute feature for incoming phone calls, as it inadvertently triggered on notifications and messages (like WhatsApp) as well.
v2.1
• Added: Light/dark mode interface.
• Added: Bluetooth microphone PTT button scanning and mapping (still buggy but we are making progress).
• Added: QSO note QRZ search will now also give the beam heading and distance from your QTH. The approximate timezone will be printed in the bottom of the QSO notepad window.
• Added: QRZ viewer, you can now visualize within TCI remote the QRZ page.
• Fixed: A back key while the SPOT window is open will now return to the main screen of TCI remote.
• Added: A back key from the main screen of TCI remote will now prompt "do you want to exit?".
• Modified: Some of the layouts, exit button, buttons under the pan adapter.
• Added: In config you can now configure up to 3 web shortcuts for quick lookups from within TCI Remote. This is very handy if you use for example web controllable devices like rotors, amplifiers, antenna switches, etc. Fill in their web address here and give it a name. A restart is required. The shortcut will pop up under the pan adapter as a little world icon.
• Added: Processor monitoring for detailed monitoring the processor use by TCI Remote. Toggle on and off in config.
• Added: Audio Jitter Buffer (Underrun Recovery) slider in config to compensate slow internet connections.
• Added: Microphone TX Buffer slider in config to instruct Thetis to buffer the transmission to prevent voice dropouts.
• Added: Automated muting of TCI remote RX stream when a phone call (or WhatsApp call) comes in.
• And many more minor fixes and security improvements.
v2.0
• Added: A whole spot viewer feature including rendering over the panadapter. Spot filtering by mode, continent, timeout and more.
• Added: Spot ticker on frequency. If the spot ticker is enabled, even if you turn off the spot rendering in the panadapter, the footer of the waterfall will display the callsign of any station that matches your current frequency.
• Added: Map any PTT button in the config window. It also has a scanning feature — push any button to detect which key has been pressed and assign it as a PTT key. Broad support for Bluetooth devices and Bluetooth PTT speaker microphones.
• Modified: Connect/disconnect and Reset are now actual icon buttons. The power button can turn Thetis on and off.
• Added: QRZ.com credentials in the config screen. Enables automatic callsign lookup and a search button in the QSO note window. Displays the station's first name and country. When an email address is found on QRZ, the REC and VDO record buttons become available.
• Added: Audio recording (MP3, max 10 min). Records the RX audio and optionally the microphone. Save, share, or email the recording directly from the app.
• Added: Video recording (WebM/MP4, max 3 min). Captures the panadapter, waterfall and S-meter in a 1280×720 frame with audio. Includes a branded overlay. When the recording finishes or is stopped, you can discard, share, download, or email the file. If Gmail is selected, the email address and attachment are pre-filled — just press Send. If another app is selected, the address is copied to the clipboard ready to paste.
• Added: Light mode toggle in the config screen. Useful on devices used in bright sunlight.
• And many more features to come…
v1.3.2
• Reworked address popup. It now stores the 3 last successful connections. The ws:// prefix is no longer required.
• Changed the Reset button. It now fully disconnects and reconnects.
• Improved Split mode. VFO B now lights up red. It automatically offsets VFO B by +5 kHz above 10 MHz, and −5 kHz below 10 MHz.
• Added clickable frequency digits for VFO A and B. Click a digit to open an up and down control menu.
• Added push and hold functionality to the arrows near the frequency counter and tune slider for continuous tuning.
• Added a SWAP button to exchange frequencies between VFO A and B.
• Added hardware PTT support for the SONIM XP PRO button and microphone down button. Thanks to KO6DLV.
• Improved the signal meter. It calculates values accurately based on bandpass filter data.
• Split signal meter calibration for lower and higher bands.
• Disabled frequency tuning by clicking the Panadapter and waterfall. This prevents conflicts with bandpass sliding in Thetis.
• Added PF (Panafall) mode options. ON keeps the visual spectrum active. RX only disables it during TX to save bandwidth. OFF disables it permanently. A virtual spectrometer is active during TX when the TCI stream is paused.
• Added Squelch control. Click the SQUELCH title to toggle it. The title turns red when active.
• Reduced the font size for VFO B. Both VFOs now fit on a single row on low resolution screens.
• Added extra bandpass filter options: 3.4k, 3.6k, 10k, and 12k.
• Fixed an issue where VFO B did not follow the VFO A mode.
• Added a visual warning. The interface glows red when the SWR exceeds 2.0.
• Added a Config button.
• Fixed the audio burst when switching from RX to TX. The app forces a mute during the first 300 ms of TX.
• Added an adjustable RX to TX mute timer in the Config screen to prevent audio feedback.
• Added an ANF button next to the NR buttons.
• Added a PTT lock timer control in the Config screen.
• Added PTT lock timer settings in config screen.
• Fixed where older Android versions didn't allow filling in the address due to keyboard flickering.
• Added Roger Beep. A configurable tone is sent after releasing PTT. Duration (200–500 ms) and frequency (1000–1200 Hz) are adjustable in the Config screen.

🔌 Want no port forwarding & 95% less bandwidth?
Use TCI Remote Compactor on your shack PC.
Get it →
📡 Connect via TCI Remote Compactor Zero-Config Tunnel
This feature requires the TCI Remote Compactor software running on your radio PC. No port forwarding or router changes needed.
Watch setup tutorial 🔌 Download Compactor

Fill in your Ham/CB call sign and the PIN code. These need to exactly match what you configured in the TCI Remote Compactor.

⚙️ COMPACTOR
►
ℹ️ Connect to the Compactor port (default :50002), not Thetis directly (:50001). Example: 192.168.1.47:50002
🔒 Connect with the Master password to configure the compactor remotely.
MASTER
— clients
CONNECTED CLIENTS
—
Callsign is set in Config ⚙ → STATION.
—
Receive-only access — no PTT, no tune. ✕ removes the password entirely.
—
Full radio control — PTT and tune allowed. ✕ removes the password entirely.
—
Changing this affects your next login. ✕ disables master access entirely.
Takes effect immediately after saving.
When enabled, mic audio is Opus-compressed before sending — reduces TX upload bandwidth. Takes effect immediately after saving.
When enabled, all connected users hear each other when transmitting. Volume is controlled by the AF Gain slider. Only works via compactor.
Shows a 20-second overlay on the waterfall when another user connects to the compactor. Master access only.
Host and port of the Thetis TCI server the compactor connects to. New connections use the updated value immediately.
🎙️ PTT & TRANSMIT
▼
Standard: press and hold to transmit, double-tap to lock. One-click lock: every tap on PTT toggles the transmitter on or off (with safety timer).
490 ms
Prevents the microphone from transmitting the tail end of the RX audio (speaker burst) when you press PTT. Default is 490 ms.
3 min
1m2m3m5m10m
Maximum TX time when PTT is locked (double-tap). The radio automatically returns to RX after this duration.
7s
DIS1s – 30sOFF
DIS = TUN/ATU buttons disabled. 1–30s = auto-cancel timer. OFF = no timer (manual stop). Default: 7s.
⚙ ADVANCED — PTT Echo Guard
Changing this value can affect PTT key behaviour. Leave at the default (1 s) unless you experience PTT flickering or the transmitter re-activating unexpectedly after releasing PTT.
1 s
Off500ms1s1.5s2s
After releasing PTT, TCI Remote ignores any incoming trx:true messages from Thetis for this duration. This absorbs delayed echo messages that would otherwise re-activate the transmitter after PTT release. Default: 1 s.
Maps the Android Volume Down key as a PTT button. Disabled by default.
Map any key or BLE device as PTT. HID Bluetooth buttons and BLE GATT devices both supported.
No key mapped.
Mapped button behaviour
Standard: hold to transmit. One-click lock: each press toggles the transmitter on or off (uses PTT lock max. time timer).
Sends a tone after releasing PTT. Disabled by default.
350 ms
200750
2350 Hz
7502800
Off
Follow RX filter bandwidth (low cut 100 Hz)
LOW CUT 100 Hz
50200300400500 Hz
HIGH CUT 2800 Hz
1k3.5k6k8.7k12k Hz
Passband: 100 – 2800 Hz
Adjusts the TX bandpass filter edges. Use presets for quick settings or the sliders for fine control. Default is 100 Hz. Off = Thetis manages the TX filter.
Which TX profile do you want to use with TCI Remote?
(You can only select a profile if you are already connected TCI remote to the server)
ℹ️ When you disconnect, TCI Remote will automatically restore the original TX profile that was active on the Thetis server.
Original profile: —
Use a different TX profile optimized for your phone's microphone. The original profile is restored when you disconnect.
Which TX profile to use when mode is FM (NFM)?
(You can only select a profile if you are already connected to the server)
ℹ️ Activates automatically when you switch to FM/NFM mode. Restores the previous profile when you switch away.
Use a dedicated TX profile for FM. Switches automatically when selecting FM/NFM mode.
🔊 AUDIO
►
20 ms
20ms200ms
Adjusts how much "head-start" your audio stream is given when a dropout occurs. Higher values (e.g. 100ms) prevent unstable networks from causing repetitive dropouts.
50 ms
50ms300ms
Instructs Thetis to buffer your incoming microphone audio. Increase if your transmitted voice stutters.
When packet loss causes the buffer to grow, TCI Remote silently removes the quietest ~1 ms window from each packet to drain it back down. At >120 ms lag: +4% speed — at >220 ms: +8% speed. Pitch is fully preserved — only duration shortens. The lag display shows +4% or +8% while active.
📡 DISPLAY & PERFORMANCE
►
Default is off. Only use this if the signals appear mirrored. This fixes the display in TCI Remote without breaking your sidebands in Thetis.
Peak is the default and matches standard Thetis ballistics. Max Bin is useful for measuring single-tone CW signals.
Adjusts the base calibration of the S-meter reading.
Off
OffFull BW
Renders additional waterfall data beyond the visible zoom window. When you tune up or down, pre-rendered areas will already have waterfall history instead of a black screen. Higher values use more CPU and memory. Off = only render visible area. Full BW = render the entire 192 kHz bandwidth (default).

If you have a slow phone, please lower the bandwidth or turn it off.
Displays the main thread CPU load percentage as an overlay in the bottom-left corner of the waterfall. High usage suggests device throttling or excessive panadapter framerates.
Check this to use the new vfo_swap_ex command. Uncheck if you are running an older version of Thetis.
When enabled, activating the VFO Lock will also disable the AF Gain slider to prevent accidental volume changes.
14%
Opaque20% transparent
Makes the QSO Note window semi-transparent so you can see the waterfall behind it. 0% = fully opaque (default). Useful when working crowded bands and you want to keep an eye on the spectrum while taking notes.
Display Mode
Tap to cycle: 🌙 Dark → ☀️ Light → 🌞 Sun → 🔆 Sun2 → 🎨 Custom. Select Custom to configure the color theme below.

📋 STATION & LOGGING
►
Latitude
Longitude
Your station coordinates in decimal degrees. Used to calculate beam heading to the worked station after a QRZ lookup.
Username
Password
Used for automatic callsign lookup in the QSO Note (🔍 button). Credentials are stored locally on your device only.
Callsign (used for some clusters)
Data Source
Select your preferred source for DX spots. Live WebSocket connections provide instant updates but require internet.
Define up to 5 links that open in an overlay. Useful for rotor controls, smart plugs, or amplifiers.
Examples (Valid addresses):
🎬 VIDEO RECORDING
►
📡 What is Video Recording?
TCI Remote can record a short video clip of your QSO — showing the panadapter, waterfall and S-meter — with the received audio embedded. This is great for sharing a rare DX contact or an interesting signal with your friends on WhatsApp, Telegram, email or social media.
How to use it
1 Open the QSO Note (the notepad icon). Type the callsign of the station you are working and tap the 🔍 search button.
2 TCI Remote looks up the callsign on QRZ.com. If an email address is found for that station, a small ✉ mailbox icon appears in the QSO Note and the ⏺ VDO button becomes available. (QRZ credentials are required — configure them in Station & Logging.)
3 Tap ⏺ VDO to start recording. The app records up to 3 minutes of panadapter, waterfall, S-meter and received audio. A pulsing dot shows recording is active.
4 Tap VDO again to stop (or it stops automatically at 3 minutes). A dialog appears with four options:
📧 Email — opens Gmail (or your default mail app) with the station's email already filled in. Just tap Send.
↗ Share — share the video via WhatsApp, Telegram, or any app on your phone.
⬇ Save — save the video file to your phone's local storage.
✕ Discard — delete the recording without saving.
💡 If you choose Email but want to use a different address, the email address is also copied to your clipboard automatically.
⚠ Performance requirement
Video rendering is CPU-intensive. A modern, powerful smartphone is recommended for smooth recording at HD quality. On slower phones the video may be choppy or the app may stutter during recording. Use a lower resolution if you experience problems.
If the recording is choppy or the app stutters, lower this setting. The video quality is reduced but the recording will be smoother.
⚠️ SECRET CONFIGURATION — use with care
►
Allows TX outside amateur bands. Intended for transverter use. Use responsibly.
⚠ Legal disclaimer
The 11-meter band (26–28 MHz) is not an amateur radio allocation. In most countries, transmitting on this band without the appropriate CB licence or type-approved equipment is illegal. By enabling this option you accept full personal responsibility for any use of this frequency range. The developer of TCI Remote accepts no liability whatsoever.
🎛️ EXPERIMENTAL AUDIO — advanced
►
Forces the app to reload and applies all updated configuration settings immediately.
🔧 Experimental Audio Settings
Tweaks at your own risk · Changes require RX/TX restart
📥 RX OUTPUT LIMITER
Threshold
Level above which the limiter starts compressing. Lower = more limiting. Default: -2 dB
Knee
How gradual the transition into compression is. 0 = hard knee, higher = softer. Default: 5 dB
Ratio
Amount of gain reduction. 20:1 = heavy limiting, 1:1 = no effect. Default: 20:1
Attack
How fast the limiter reacts to loud signals. Lower = faster response. Default: 5 ms
Release
How fast the limiter stops compressing after signal drops. Lower = snappier. Default: 50 ms
📥 RX AUDIO PIPELINE
Analyser Smoothing
Smoothing for the audio level meter. Higher = smoother but slower response. Default: 89%
CW Anti-Squarewave Filter
A sharp Low-Pass filter that mathematically removes harsh 3rd/5th harmonics from digitally clipped audio. Set to ~1000Hz when listening to CW to kill the "buzz". 0 = OFF
⚠️ Turn this OFF for SSB (Voice) or your audio will sound completely muffled! Re-connect TCI to apply.
Fade Envelope
Crossfade length at audio chunk boundaries to prevent clicks. Higher = smoother but adds latency. Default: 64 samples
Jitter Buffer
Target buffer depth for incoming audio. Higher = more resilient to network jitter but more delay. Default: 100 ms
Lag Reset Threshold
When buffered audio exceeds this, the buffer resets to prevent excessive delay. Default: 750 ms
📤 TX MICROPHONE PIPELINE
TX Buffer (TCI)
Server-side buffering for your mic audio. Higher = more resilient but adds TX delay. Default: 50 ms
WS Congestion Limit
Max WebSocket send queue before mic frames are dropped. Prevents runaway buffering on slow connections. Default: 64 kB
Sample Clamp
Hard clip ceiling for mic output samples. Lower = prevents overdriving but reduces dynamic range. Default: 100%
TCI Audio Samples
Number of samples per TCI audio frame. Smaller = lower latency but more CPU and network overhead. Default: 2048
Browser-level audio processing applied to the raw microphone input before TCI Remote processes it. Off by default for maximum audio fidelity. Requires TX restart to take effect. Default: all OFF
🎚️ SOFTWARE MIC AGC
Target RMS
Desired average loudness of your voice. AGC adjusts gain to keep speech at this level. Default: 20% (~-14 dBFS)
Noise Gate
Below this level, input is considered silence and gain is frozen. Prevents gain from rising during pauses. If you are in noisy environments, set the value higher to filter the background noise out. Setting the slider too high might cut out parts of your speech. Default: 8/1000 (~-42 dBFS)
Attack (gain×)
Gain multiplier per frame when input is too loud. Lower = faster gain reduction. Default: 82%
Release (gain×)
Gain multiplier per frame when input is too quiet. Higher = faster gain recovery. Default: 10010/10000
Hold Time
After speech drops below the noise gate, gain is frozen for this duration before normal AGC resumes. Default: 400 ms
Min Gain
Lowest gain the AGC will apply. Prevents the mic from being muted entirely by the AGC. Default: 5%
Max Gain
Highest gain the AGC will apply. Prevents excessive amplification of background noise. Default: 150%
🧪 ANTI-JITTER LAB
These experimental controls target the most common causes of audio clicking, popping, and jittering in WebSocket-based audio streams. Each addresses a different layer of the problem. Changes require an RX audio restart.
AudioContext Latency Hint
Controls how the browser prioritizes audio processing. "interactive" gives the lowest latency but uses more CPU and battery. "playback" gives the browser more room to buffer, reducing glitches on slower devices at the cost of ~50-100ms extra delay. Try "playback" if you hear jitter on older phones. Default: interactive
The DynamicsCompressor (limiter) in the audio chain has its own attack/release timing that can cause subtle pumping artifacts — a rhythmic volume fluctuation that sounds like jitter. With a -2 dB threshold and 20:1 ratio, it reacts to nearly everything. Bypassing it removes this processing entirely, sending raw audio straight from the analyser to speakers. Try this if jitter persists even with good network. Default: OFF (limiter active)
Each audio chunk creates a BufferSourceNode. Without cleanup, hundreds of dead nodes accumulate, increasing garbage collector (GC) pressure. When the GC runs, it pauses JavaScript, creating an audible click. This option disconnects nodes immediately after they finish playing, keeping memory lean and GC pauses minimal. Disable only for debugging. Default: ON
Instead of multiplying each audio sample by a fade envelope in JavaScript (main thread), this uses a Web Audio GainNode with linearRampToValueAtTime() which runs entirely on the audio render thread. This means fade processing cannot be disrupted by main-thread activity (waterfall drawing, DX cluster, DOM updates). The tradeoff: each chunk gets its own GainNode, slightly increasing node count. Best combined with Node Cleanup ON. Default: OFF
GainNode Fade Duration
Duration of the GainNode fade-in and fade-out at chunk boundaries. Only used when GainNode fade is ON. Shorter = less audible gap masking. Longer = smoother transitions but may muffle transients. The Web Audio spec recommends 2-5ms for click elimination. Default: 2 ms
Gap Resync Delay
When the playback schedule falls behind real time (a gap), this is how far into the future the next chunk is scheduled. Lower = tighter recovery (less silence) but risks scheduling in the past if the device is slow. Higher = safer but introduces a brief audible silence gap. If you hear periodic clicks, try increasing this. Default: 20 ms
Chunk Overlap
Schedules the next chunk slightly before the previous one ends, creating a tiny crossfade overlap. This fills micro-gaps caused by floating-point duration rounding errors or sample rate mismatches between server and client. Too much overlap causes a "reverb" or "phasing" effect. Start with 0.5ms and increase only if you hear tiny clicks between chunks. Default: 0 ms (no overlap)
⚠️ These are advanced parameters. Incorrect values may cause audio distortion, dropouts, or high CPU usage. Use the RESET button to restore factory defaults. Changes to browser mic constraints (Echo/Noise/AGC) require a TX restart.
Help
⚠ IMPORTANT INFORMATION — READ THIS BEFORE USING v3.4. HOLD THE BUTTON AT THE BOTTOM FOR 3 SECONDS TO CONFIRM YOU HAVE READ THIS.
Welcome to TCI Remote v3.4
🚨 IMPORTANT THETIS SETTING to be done before proceeding:

You are strongly required to configure the Time Out Timer in Thetis to provide a hardware-level failsafe against your radio being stuck in TX.
WARNING: Without this setting, your radio equipment runs the risk of remaining stuck in transmission and being damaged in the event of a network outage!

Setup → General → Options → Options 2 → Time Out Timers
Please set it between 180 seconds and 300 seconds.
🚨 Compactor Upgrade Required:
v3.4 requires TCI Remote Compactor v1.3.2 or newer to enable all new features.
Please upgrade the Compactor on your host PC before connecting. Without the update, some features will not work correctly.
What's New in v3.4:
  • Fixed: General stability
  • Fixed: Recall last frequency/mode/RX filter per band
  • Fixed: Web shortcuts provided by RN radios now properly displayed per station
  • Fixed: Signal meter calibration
  • Added: Manual offset possible for signal meter
Audio Tip: If you are experiencing audio stuttering, please give unrestricted battery access (allow background activity) for the app on your phone. It will help a lot to avoid stuttering in the audio.
⭐ Your contribution will help make the app better!
Any donation is welcome. A few euros to you makes a big difference to cover the cost and time for the development of this free app.
💳 Click here to Donate via PayPal
Hold the button below for 3 seconds to confirm you have read this.
